channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Steam Controller Being Listed On Ebay For Double And Triple The Price
item.content:encoded:
The Steam Controller has been on the market for only a day, but it quickly sold out at every retailer.

The response has been so strong that Valve made a post on social media stating that it’s “working on getting more in stock and will have an update on expected timeline soon.” But because of the high demand for the controller, that means that some people are looking to take advantage of those who want one to actually use.

A visit to the auction site eBay shows people listing the Steam Controller for double, and sometimes triple, the $99 retail price.

Some have opened up bidding, with bids starting at $110. Others, have listed “Buy It Now” prices of $299.99 and even $399.99. But don’t worry, they do say “or best offer”, so you can try and get it for less.

Again, Valve says they’re working on getting more controllers in stoke, though a timetable isn’t currently known. Still, you’re better off just waiting a little while longer rather than pay two, three, or even four times the cost for the controller.

What do you think of people reselling the Steam Controller for double or even triple its cost? Leave your thoughts down in the comments and in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about Call of Duty no longer releasing on last-gen platforms. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: League of Legends Patch 26.10 Preview: Gluttonous Greaves Nerfs
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has revealed what changes are coming to League of Legends in LoL Patch 26.10 next week (May 13).

Earlier this year, the publisher confirmed that LoL will receive smaller midseason patches this year.

And Patch 26.10 is one of them, with just nine champion changes scheduled for the update so far.

Lead gameplay designer Matt ‘Phroxzon’ Leung-Harrison revealed the upcoming changes, but only added context to some item and rune changes that are coming to the game.

LoL Patch 26.10 Champion Buffs and Nerfs

Champion Buffs

  • Ambessa
  • Galio
  • Wukong
  • Zeri

Champion Nerfs

  • Anivia
  • Ashe
  • Naafiri
  • Shyvana
  • Zed

Champion Adjustments

  • Lee Sin
  • Quinn

Phroxzon Explains Upcoming Item and Rune Changes

Phroxzon claims that the biggest changes this patch will be to the nerfs to the Deathfire Touch rune and the Gluttonous Greaves item.

Deathfire was already nerfed a few days ago in a micropatch, with the adaptive damage per second nerfed.

Due to this prior nerf, Phroxzon revealed that this next change will only be a small one, Phroxzon explained his mixed feelings with the rune, saying:

“Deathfire is looking a little too strong, especially in the laning case, but the audience it’s landed on has been great in giving some of these champs a keystone they’re actually happy with.”

Meanwhile, Phroxzon revealed that the Gluttonous Greaves boots are becoming too popular:

“Gluttonous Greaves has picked up a nice new user base, especially Fighters and AD Assassins who might not have another offensive boot option (outside of Lucidity).

“We think the boots are too strong, both in non-mid roles and in mid with the boot upgrade.”

What buffs or nerfs do you want to see in League of Legends? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And while there aren’t many major changes this patch, LoL has undergone some surprising updates this year. Especially the added ability to play LoL with a controller following the release of WASD controls.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Enemies Adapt to Repetitive PlayStyles
item.content:encoded:
Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced developers have revealed new combat details, introducing new adaptive enemies, the core combat loop, advanced attacks, and more.

The highly anticipated remake, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, is set to release in a couple of months, and fans are curious about the improvements and changes the developers have made to the game. Last, we were informed that the ‘Hidden Blade’ will not be a selectable weapon. Now they have provided in-depth information about combat.

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Combat Features Revealed

A new post on the Assassin’s Creed blog provides players with combat details for Black Flag, resynced from Paul Fu, the Creative Director. Fu explained that the game’s core combat loop revolves around takedowns, bringing enemies to a state where you can end them with a finisher. There are multiple types of takedowns, which players can learn more about below:

Takedowns

  • Hidden Blade Takedown: Breaking an enemy’s defense with multiple attacks will trigger a Hidden Blade takedown
  • Perfect Parry Takedowns: Triggered by timing a Perfect Parry, Edward can chain his takedowns to up to four nearby enemies, depending on the sword he carries.
  • Wall Takedown: Kick an enemy into a wall to initiate a fatal takedown.
  • Ground Takedown: Grounded enemies (from an explosion or Sweep move) can be killed by a Ground Takedown

The Hidden Blade will bring more visual variations to takedowns once unlocked in the story, along with different animation variations like Chain Takedowns.

Using the Environment

The environment is a big part of combat in Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, bringing fatal kicks into play from ledges, corridors being the spot for chain takedowns, exploding powder kegs, and wall-takedowns through well-placed kicks.

New Dodge Attack and Other Advanced Attacks of Black Flag Resynced

The developers have informed players that Black Flag Resynced’s combat will introduce a new dodge attack that can deal additional damage when timed right, turning it into a perfect dodge. The heavy attack is also new and can be performed by holding down the attack button. Heavy attacks differ according to the weapon you are using:

Rapier: Heavy Strikes are more damaging and can pierce through foes.
Cutlass: Heavy Strikers cover a wide area, hitting multiple enemies.
Pistol-Sword: Two damaging shots are fired, allowing Edward to focus fire on one enemy or spread the damage on two.

Black Flag Resynced Introduces Adaptive Enemies

Another new introduction to combat in Black Flag Resynced is adaptive enemies, who can “react to repetitive behavior”, making players change their approach from time to time. Paul Fu explained:

“For example, wait around too much for a Parry, and enemies will react by performing Unstoppable Attacks that cannot be parried. Abuse a Kick too much, and enemies will quickly dodge them. The trick is to alternate between offense and defense. Varying your combos helps. Rotate between Kicks, Sweeps, Rope Darts, Pistols and Heavy Strikes at the end of your attack combos to confuse enemies.”

Advanced Enemy Archetypes

Last but not least, introducing advanced enemy archetypes will require Edward to adopt different strategies to face them. Players will find archetypes like Brute, Captain, and Demolitionist, who can deny takedowns, have heavy defense, perform unblockable attacks, and more.

Players will have to use different tools and attacks from Edward’s arsenal to face these enemies, including the Rope Dart, a Sweeping move, a pistol shot, using the Gun Kata, and more.

In other news, Ubisoft has revealed that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced has already surpassed 1 million wishlists. Additionally, check out the minimum, recommended, high, and ultra PC system requirements for Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced. What are your thoughts on all the new Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced combat details? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about more Microsoft CoreAI folks joining leadership at Xbox. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Xbox Leadership Changes Introduce More Former CoreAI Folks
item.content:encoded:
Asha Sharma, the new face of Xbox, has made a slew of changes to the leadership model in and around the brand of late. Today, another sweep of new hires was revealed, along with some high-profile departures and even a promotion or two.

What might have some Team Green fans looking on with bated breath is the number of high-ranking employees coming over from Microsoft CoreAI, the department within the tech titan from which Asha Sharma hailed, before landing her Xbox spot.

But There’s No Indication It’s a Bad Thing

In a report published by The Verge, it was confirmed that several leadership changes have happened at Xbox. In recent weeks, Asha Sharma has been making mad waves, taking steps to reduce the cost of Game Pass, drop the Microsoft Gaming branding, and push for more discussions around exclusivity.

The report summarises the key changes:

  • Jared Palmer (former CoreAI) enters as VP of Engineering and Technical Advisor to Sharma
  • Tim Allen (former CoreAI) enters to ‘lead design’ and ‘bring together product design, design engineering, research, and creative with a fan-first focus.’
  • Jonathan McKay (former CoreAI) enters as growth, data platform, and analytics lead
  • Evan Chaki (former CoreAI) enters to lead ‘a forward-deployed engineering group focused on removing repetitive work, simplifying development, and improving how we operate.’

The report from The Verge stresses that there are no alarm bells sounding for a huge AI push, as that’s not what seems to be happening here. It’s quite the opposite, in fact. Days ago, Sharma made clear the company’s intentions where AI is concerne, and this push is more about increasing the technical stock around the higher echelons of Xbox.

Elsewhere, Roanne Sones, corporate VP of Xbox Devices, is taking a leave of absence, while Kevin Gammill, VP of Xbox UX, is stepping down after almost two decades at the corporation.

Jason Ronald, the VP of Next Gen, is being promoted. Per a statement from Sharma:

On hardware, we are elevating Jason Ronald, accountable for Project Helix, and our platform.

This is an important time for Xbox. Our goal with this change is simple: build a platform that is affordable, personal, and open by staying close to the work and the people we serve. We will continue to add the capabilities needed to get there

Do you think these new hires spell great things for Xbox? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Battlefield 6’s season three update adds a huge map

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Can You Play Subnautica 2 Early Access Before Launch? Xbox Game Pass Status, Explained
item.content:encoded:
We know that Subnautica 2 is going into early access for a decent length of time, but we’ll check if you can play early access even earlier, and whether you can play it for free via a free Xbox Game Pass subscription.

Two big questions to answer for you today: Does the early access have early access to let you play earlier than early access (was that fun to read?), and if Subnautica 2 is an Xbox Game Pass benefit. With Xbox heading in the right direction, this would be a huge coup.

Will Subnautica 2 Early Access Be Available Before Release?

There is no way to get your hands on Subnautica 2 before it goes live in early access.

  • More Insider Gaming: Subnautica 2 Early Access Price & All Editions

Believe me, we’ve all seen it: Early access games with even early access. It was a sensible question asked about Subnautica 2, but it’s not the case here. Developer and publisher Unkonon Worlds Entertainment has made it clear that early access begins on May 14 for all players.

Speaking of which, make sure you know how long Subnautica 2 will be in early access for, along with news of its 1.0 release in the future.

Is Subnautica 2 Early Access on Xbox Game Pass?

You need either a Game Pass Ultimate or PC Game Pass subscription to be able to play Subnautica 2 for free through Xbox Game Pass.

Subnautica 2 is part of the Xbox Game Pass May 2026 Wave 1 Titles. With Xbox’s CEO addressing pricing missteps, Microsoft has been on a warpath to fix its image.

One thing that isn’t changing too much is the subscription service adding high-quality games to its library.

  • More Insider Gaming: Subnautica 2 Early Access Release Date, Times & Platforms

It remains to be seen if Subnautica 2 ends up being of this calibre, but as long as people have the right subscription, early access is going to be extremely popular!

Will you be playing Subnautica 2 Early Access on Xbox or PC? Is it worth getting Game Pass to save money on the full price? If you’re unsure, why not discuss it with other Insider Gaming community members via our free-to-join Discord Server?

The news and content don’t stop there; we know it’s hard to keep up with everything, so if you missed it, read about why Subnautica 2 was actually delayed in the first place.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton
